meaning: adj.后面的,(两者中)后者的,较后的,近来的
examples: The latter point is the most important.
He tracked down his cousin and uncle. The latter was sick.
The exercises can be fun and a good warm-up for the latter part of the programme.
More than half think the Fed will hike rates in the latter half of the year.
The latter will utilize the sun that shines here for most of the year.
